en.extract:
The Fiji streaked fantail (Rhipidura layardi) is a species of bird in the family Rhipiduridae that is endemic to the islands of Fiji. It was formerly considered to be subspecies of the streaked fantail (now the New Caledonian streaked fantail). Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests.


Taxonomy
The binomial name Rhipidura layardi was introduced in 1877 by the Italian zoologist Tommaso Salvadori as a replacement name for Rhipidura albogularis. This earlier name had been introduced by the naturalist Edgar Leopold Layard in 1875 based on a specimen collected on the island of Ovalau in the Fiji islands. The specific epithet albogulari was preoccupied as it had been used in 1831 in the combination Muscicapa (Muscylva) albogularis by the French naturalist René Lesson for the white-spotted fantail.
Three subspecies are recognised:

R. l. rufilateralis Sharpe, 1879 – Taveuni (central north Fiji)
R. l. layardi Salvadori, 1877 – Viti Levu and Ovalau (Lomaiviti group; west Fiji)
R. l. erythronota Sharpe, 1879 – Vanua Levu and Yanganga (north of northwest Vanua Levu; central north Fiji)


References

The '''Fiji streaked fantail''' ('''''Rhipidura layardi''''') is a [[species]] of bird in the family [[Rhipiduridae]] that is endemic to the islands of [[Fiji]]. It was formerly considered to be [[subspecies]] of the streaked fantail (now the [[New Caledonian streaked fantail]]). Its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical moist lowland [[forest]]s.

==Taxonomy==
The [[binomial name]] ''Rhipidura layardi'' was introduced in 1877 by the Italian zoologist [[Tommaso Salvadori]] as a replacement name for ''Rhipidura albogularis''.<ref>{{ cite journal | last=Salvadori | first=Tommaso | author-link=Tommaso Salvadori | year=1877 | title=Notes on two birds from the Fiji Islands | journal=Ibis | pages=142–796 [143] | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/8801667 }}</ref> This earlier name had been introduced by the naturalist [[Edgar Leopold Layard]] in 1875 based on a specimen collected on the island of [[Ovalau]] in the [[Fiji]] islands.<ref>{{ cite journal | last=Layard | first=Edgar Leopole | author-link=Edgar Leopold Layard | year=1875 | title=Orthological notes from Fiji, with descriptions of supposed new species of birds | journal=Proceedings of the Zoological Society of London | volume=1875 | pages=27–30 [29] | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/28501176 }}</ref><ref>{{ cite journal | last=Layard | first=Edgar Leopole | author-link=Edgar Leopold Layard | year=1875 | title=Notes on Fijian birds | journal=Proceedings of the Zoological Society of London | volume=1875 | pages=423–442 [434] | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/28501677 }}</ref> The specific epithet ''albogulari'' was preoccupied as it had been used in 1831 in the combination ''Muscicapa (Muscylva) albogularis'' by the French naturalist [[René Lesson]] for the [[white-spotted fantail]].<ref>{{ cite book | last=Lesson | first=René P. | author-link=René Lesson | year=1831 | editor-last=Bélanger | editor-first=Charles | title=Voyage aux Indes-orientales, par le nord de l'Europe | volume=3: Zoologie | language=French | location=Paris | publisher=Arthus Bertrand  | pages=264–265 | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=3fiRW86wPKwC&pg=PA264 }}</ref><ref>{{ cite book | editor1-last=Mayr | editor1-first=Ernst | editor1-link=Ernst Mayr | editor2-last=Cottrell | editor2-first=G. William | year=1986 | title=Check-List of Birds of the World | volume=11 | publisher=Museum of Comparative Zoology | place=Cambridge, Massachusetts | page=548 | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/14484249 }}</ref>

Three [[subspecies]] are recognised:<ref name=ioc>{{cite web| editor1-last=Gill | editor1-first=Frank | editor1-link=Frank Gill (ornithologist) | editor2-last=Donsker | editor2-first=David | editor3-last=Rasmussen | editor3-first=Pamela | editor3-link=Pamela C. Rasmussen | date=December 2023 | title=Orioles, drongos, fantails | work=IOC World Bird List Version 14.1 | url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/orioles/ | publisher=International Ornithologists' Union | access-date=15 January 2024}}</ref>
* ''R. l. rufilateralis'' [[Richard Bowdler Sharpe|Sharpe]], 1879 – [[Taveuni]] (central north [[Fiji]])
* ''R. l. layardi'' [[Tommaso Salvadori|Salvadori]], 1877 – [[Viti Levu]] and [[Ovalau]] (Lomaiviti group; west [[Fiji]])
* ''R. l. erythronota'' Sharpe, 1879 – [[Vanua Levu]] and Yanganga (north of northwest [[Vanua Levu]]; central north [[Fiji]])
